tasks.title: Freeze Exposure Scenario MCP architecture, scope, and release gates
tasks.description: Establish the module's strict external-dose boundary, supported v0.1 scenario classes, semantic conventions, and repository skeleton before implementation begins.
tasks.details: Translate the PRD into explicit ADRs and an implementation charter. Publish decisions covering module purpose, non-goals (no PBPK, no internal exposure, no BER, no final risk characterization), deterministic-first strategy for v0.1, plugin architecture by scenario family, and the requirement that every scenario expose assumptions and provenance explicitly. Scaffold the repository with src/exposure_scenario_mcp/, plugins/, defaults/, schemas/, tests/, docs/, and config/. Define release gates for benchmark replication, unit/route semantics, PBPK handoff compatibility, and failure-mode transparency.
tasks.testStrategy: Review ADRs and release-gate criteria with the stack owners and confirm the repository skeleton, docs, and CI baseline reflect the agreed boundary.

subtasks.title: Codify module ownership and exclusions
subtasks.description: Document exactly what the module does and does not own.
subtasks.details: Create docs/adr/0001-boundary.md defining in-scope responsibilities: external dose construction, deterministic screening scenarios, basic aggregate/co-use, inhalation screening, scenario comparison, and PBPK handoff export. Explicitly exclude internal exposure, PBPK execution, PoD generation, WoE, BER, and final risk decisions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Cross-check the ADR against the PRD and confirm README, roadmap, and backlog all reflect the same exclusions.

subtasks.title: Adopt deterministic-first v0.1 strategy
subtasks.description: Fix the execution philosophy for the first release.
subtasks.details: Record the decision that v0.1 prioritizes deterministic consumer screening, basic aggregate/co-use, and inhalation screening rather than full probabilistic population engines. Capture the rationale: avoid false precision, keep validation tractable, and preserve a clean later extension path.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that the roadmap and planned tool catalog do not accidentally depend on deferred probabilistic functionality.

subtasks.title: Define supported scenario classes and semantic tags
subtasks.description: Freeze the scenario taxonomy used across the module.
subtasks.details: Publish a scenario-class matrix for screening, aggregate, inhalation, and future refined/population classes. Define mandatory tags and semantics so outputs can never masquerade as a more refined class than the implemented math supports.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the taxonomy in a draft schema/example and ensure every planned tool maps to exactly one scenario class or conversion path.

subtasks.title: Define route, unit, and assumption-governance policy
subtasks.description: Create the cross-cutting semantic rules for exposure computation.
subtasks.details: Specify allowed routes, canonical units, allowed dose metrics, default-value provenance requirements, and failure rules for missing parameters. Include the principle that every default must cite a versioned source and be emitted in exposureAssumptionRecord outputs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review these rules against the PBPK handoff needs and confirm that no planned plugin uses ambiguous units or hidden defaults.

subtasks.title: Scaffold repository, CI, and baseline developer workflow
subtasks.description: Create the implementation skeleton and minimum automation required for the module.
subtasks.details: Initialize package structure, plugin folders, default-data versioning locations, lint/test setup, CI workflow, and placeholder docs. Add a baseline contract-validation command and a place for benchmark fixtures and defaults manifests.
subtasks.testStrategy: Clone and run the empty scaffold in a clean environment, verifying lint/test commands and placeholder plugin registration work.

tasks.title: Publish the Exposure Scenario MCP schema and contract layer
tasks.description: Define the complete versioned object model, route/unit conventions, and examples before implementing calculators.
tasks.details: Create the public schema pack under schemas/ and MCP response schemas under docs/contracts/schemas/. The schema layer must separate input requests, product-use profiles, population profiles, scenario outputs, aggregate outputs, PBPK handoff objects, comparison outputs, and assumption records. Every outward-facing object must carry schemaVersion, provenance, limitations, fitForPurpose, qualityFlags, and explicit scenario-class tags. Publish examples and a contract manifest so ToxClaw and PBPK MCP can introspect the module cleanly.
tasks.testStrategy: Run schema validation across all examples, ensure route/unit enumerations are enforced, and confirm the manifest references only valid schemas and examples.

subtasks.title: Define common provenance, assumption, and semantic primitives
subtasks.description: Create reusable schema fragments for all exposure-facing records.
subtasks.details: Implement shared fragments for provenance, assumption source references, uncertainty/limitation notes, route enums, unit enums, scenario-class tags, and fit-for-purpose markers. Store them in schemas/common/ and document allowed values and extension rules.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate fragments independently and ensure they can be composed by all planned schemas without ambiguity.

subtasks.title: Create ingress schemas for requests and profiles
subtasks.description: Define how scenario requests and supporting profiles enter the module.
subtasks.details: Publish productUseProfile.v1, populationProfile.v1, and exposureScenarioRequest.v1. These must capture product category, route, concentration-in-product, use amount/frequency, body weight or target population context, and declared assumption sources.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write schema tests covering valid screening requests, missing required profile fields, and invalid route/unit combinations.

subtasks.title: Create scenario and aggregate output schemas
subtasks.description: Define the core outward-facing exposure objects used by ToxClaw and PBPK MCP.
subtasks.details: Publish exposureScenario.v1, aggregateExposureSummary.v1, and exposureAssumptionRecord.v1. Require fields for scenario class, route, external dose estimate, unit, aggregation method, dominant contributors, limitations, and provenance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate examples for a deterministic screening scenario, a simple aggregate scenario, and a fully specified assumption ledger.

subtasks.title: Create PBPK handoff and comparison schemas
subtasks.description: Define the explicit contracts consumed downstream.
subtasks.details: Publish pbpkScenarioInput.v1 and scenarioComparisonRecord.v1. Ensure pbpkScenarioInput contains only the dosing semantics PBPK MCP needs, not higher-level product narratives, and that scenarioComparisonRecord can express screening-vs-refined or component-vs-aggregate differences transparently.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that PBPK handoff examples contain normalized units and route semantics and that comparison examples capture both numeric and assumption-level deltas.

subtasks.title: Publish examples and machine-readable contract manifest
subtasks.description: Make the schema layer discoverable for orchestrators and other MCPs.
subtasks.details: Create schemas/examples/ covering happy-path, edge-case, and fail-path scenarios. Publish docs/contracts/README.md and a contract manifest resource that maps tools to schemas and versions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that every published example passes schema validation and that the manifest can be consumed programmatically from a live MCP instance.

tasks.title: Build the plugin runtime, defaults registry, and provenance kernel
tasks.description: Implement the shared execution substrate for scenario plugins, default-value management, explicit error handling, and future async extension.
tasks.details: Create the plugin interface and execution kernel that route validated scenario requests to scenario-family implementations while keeping the shell lightweight. Add a versioned defaults registry that stores fact-sheet values, route-specific reference data, and source citations with immutable version IDs and hashes. Implement a provenance kernel that records which defaults were used, why, and from which versioned source. Ensure the design can later support asynchronous probabilistic jobs without breaking the v0.1 synchronous contract.
tasks.testStrategy: Run integration tests proving plugins can be registered and executed, defaults are version-resolved deterministically, and missing parameters trigger structured errors instead of silent fallbacks.

subtasks.title: Implement the scenario plugin interface and execution kernel
subtasks.description: Create the internal API every scenario family must follow.
subtasks.details: Define plugin interfaces for screening, aggregate, inhalation, and future probabilistic engines. Implement request dispatch, plugin registration, and shared execution context objects. Keep compute-layer objects lightweight compared to boundary validation models.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test plugin discovery and dispatch with mock plugins and verify that invalid plugin outputs are rejected before leaving the kernel.

subtasks.title: Build the versioned defaults registry
subtasks.description: Create the source-of-truth store for reference parameters and fact-sheet values.
subtasks.details: Implement a defaults registry with version identifiers, source citations, hashes, effective dates, and route/product applicability metadata. Store defaults in versioned data files under defaults/ and create loaders with cache controls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that the same request resolves the same defaults reproducibly and that changing a defaults version changes provenance outputs predictably.

subtasks.title: Implement provenance capture and assumption emission
subtasks.description: Make every calculation traceable at the default and parameter level.
subtasks.details: Create a provenance kernel that records explicit user inputs, resolved defaults, excluded defaults, algorithm identifiers, and plugin versions. Ensure every scenario output can emit exposureAssumptionRecord entries with source references and confidence notes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that a completed scenario result can reconstruct exactly which defaults and user-supplied values were used.

subtasks.title: Implement structured error and warning models
subtasks.description: Make missing or impossible inputs fail loudly and consistently.
subtasks.details: Create typed error payloads for missing parameters, impossible scenario combinations, route/unit mismatches, unsupported product categories, and defaults conflicts. Add warning classes for conservative approximations or downgraded outputs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Add tests showing that each failure mode returns a structured error object and no hidden fallback assumption is applied.

subtasks.title: Prepare async-compatible job abstractions for future extensions
subtasks.description: Lay groundwork for population engines without forcing async in v0.1.
subtasks.details: Add lightweight job/result abstractions and status objects that remain unused for most v0.1 calculations but keep the public contract extensible. Document when future probabilistic engines should switch to asynchronous execution.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that synchronous tools can still emit stable metadata compatible with later async upgrades and that no client breakage is introduced.

tasks.title: Implement deterministic screening scenario plugin
tasks.description: Build the core deterministic consumer exposure engine for the first release, including route-specific equations, normalized units, and explicit assumption output.
tasks.details: Implement the main screening plugin(s) for supported consumer/product use cases. Convert validated request data and resolved defaults into external dose estimates using explicit formulas per route and product class. Normalize units, document every assumption used, and tag outputs clearly as screening scenarios. Hard-fail when essential parameters are missing or incompatible with the selected route/product type. Keep the logic transparent and benchmarkable rather than over-optimized.
tasks.testStrategy: Run the screening plugin against benchmark and synthetic reference cases, checking exact or tightly bounded numeric agreement, correct unit normalization, and fully explicit assumption/provenance output.

subtasks.title: Implement route-specific screening equations
subtasks.description: Encode deterministic external-dose calculations for the supported routes and product classes.
subtasks.details: Create calculation modules for the initial supported screening routes and product types, ensuring each one uses explicit formulas and no hidden transformations. Separate route logic cleanly so additional plugins can be added later without editing the kernel.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each route-specific equation against hand-calculated examples and confirm identical results within tolerance.

subtasks.title: Normalize route semantics, units, and dose metrics
subtasks.description: Guarantee that output quantities are unambiguous and PBPK-ready later.
subtasks.details: Implement normalization utilities for product concentration, use amount, event frequency, body weight normalization, and daily dose metrics. Enforce canonical unit conversions and reject ambiguous route/unit combinations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write negative tests for invalid unit conversions, mixed units, and unsupported route/dose combinations.

subtasks.title: Resolve required parameters from inputs and defaults
subtasks.description: Fill calculation inputs deterministically while preserving provenance.
subtasks.details: Use the defaults registry and validated request objects to resolve all required parameters. Emit explicit assumption records for every default or derivation step applied to the scenario.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test cases with fully user-specified inputs and partially defaulted inputs, confirming outputs differ only where expected and assumption records capture the difference.

subtasks.title: Emit screening scenario and assumption records
subtasks.description: Map internal calculations to outward-facing contracts.
subtasks.details: Serialize the resulting deterministic calculations into exposureScenario.v1 and exposureAssumptionRecord.v1, including scenario-class tags, route, dose metric, limitations, and provenance. Ensure the output can later feed PBPK export without re-interpretation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run contract tests validating all outward-facing screening outputs and verifying required tags and provenance fields are present.

subtasks.title: Benchmark deterministic screening outputs against reference cases
subtasks.description: Prove the first scenario engine is numerically and semantically trustworthy.
subtasks.details: Add benchmark fixtures for representative consumer products and routes, compare results to reference calculations, and record allowed tolerances and known deviations. Store benchmark notes with source citations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Require the benchmark suite to pass in CI and verify that provenance outputs also match expected default-source versions.

tasks.title: Implement aggregate and co-use scenario plugin
tasks.description: Build the aggregate/co-use engine that combines multiple scenarios transparently while distinguishing additive screening logic from more refined modeling.
tasks.details: Implement a plugin that consumes multiple component scenarios or requests, combines them according to explicit aggregation rules, reports dominant contributors, and preserves all component assumptions and provenance. The plugin must clearly distinguish simple additive screening from any future refined aggregation methods, and must never present a simple aggregate result as refined without a different algorithm class. Outputs must remain auditable down to the component scenario level.
tasks.testStrategy: Test aggregate scenarios with multiple component cases, verifying numeric aggregation, contributor ranking, assumption inheritance, and explicit aggregation-method tagging. Confirm that impossible or inconsistent component combinations fail cleanly.

subtasks.title: Define aggregation semantics and method tags
subtasks.description: Create the formal language for aggregate/co-use outputs.
subtasks.details: Document supported aggregation approaches for v0.1, such as simple additive screening, and create explicit tags and descriptions for each method. Ensure the semantics prohibit method inflation, where a simple sum is misrepresented as refined aggregate modeling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review examples and confirm every aggregate output explicitly declares its method and limitations.

subtasks.title: Implement component-scenario ingestion and validation
subtasks.description: Prepare multiple scenario inputs for aggregation.
subtasks.details: Create logic that validates component scenario compatibility, harmonizes units/routes where appropriate, and rejects impossible combinations or missing key inputs. Preserve links to each component scenario and its assumption records.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test valid multi-scenario combinations and invalid combinations such as incompatible routes or missing component metadata.

subtasks.title: Compute aggregate dose estimates and contributor summaries
subtasks.description: Produce transparent aggregate outputs with traceable component contributions.
subtasks.details: Implement the aggregation math, dominant-contributor identification, and uncertainty/limitation notes specific to additive screening. Make sure component records remain addressable from the aggregate summary.
subtasks.testStrategy: Benchmark aggregate calculations against hand-worked examples and verify contributor ordering and totals.

subtasks.title: Serialize aggregate outputs and inherited assumptions
subtasks.description: Map aggregate results into public contracts with full provenance.
subtasks.details: Emit aggregateExposureSummary.v1 plus any linked exposureScenario.v1 component refs and inherited or derived assumption records. Include aggregation-method tags, dominant contributors, and explicit notes on what the aggregate does not represent.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run contract tests and provenance checks confirming all component and aggregate references are preserved.

subtasks.title: Add edge-case and failure-mode tests for aggregate logic
subtasks.description: Harden the plugin against misleading or invalid use.
subtasks.details: Create negative tests for impossible co-use assumptions, duplicate component handling, route mismatch, and malformed aggregate requests. Ensure the plugin returns structured failures rather than silent partial aggregates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute the negative test suite and confirm every failure path yields a typed error and no untagged partial result.

tasks.title: Implement inhalation screening plugin
tasks.description: Build the inhalation/aerosol screening branch with explicit route mechanics, assumptions, and output tags suitable for v0.1 screening use.
tasks.details: Implement a dedicated inhalation screening plugin rather than trying to overload generic screening math. Support the chosen v0.1 inhalation approach, including aerosol/spray screening assumptions, ventilation or room-model assumptions where applicable, and explicit particle or transfer parameters if they are part of the selected formula. Hard-fail on missing inhalation-critical parameters and tag outputs clearly as inhalation screening scenarios. Document limitations so downstream users do not mistake the outputs for refined inhalation exposure assessment.
tasks.testStrategy: Run reference inhalation screening cases and edge-case tests verifying numeric outputs, assumption records, route tags, and correct failure behavior on missing inhalation-critical inputs.

subtasks.title: Define inhalation screening parameter model
subtasks.description: Fix the minimum viable input model for inhalation scenarios.
subtasks.details: Specify required inhalation-specific inputs, defaults, and semantic rules such as aerosol fraction, room assumptions, exposure duration, or ventilation terms depending on the selected approach. Align these with schema fields and the defaults registry.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the parameter model against benchmark cases and confirm all required inputs are represented explicitly.

subtasks.title: Implement inhalation-specific calculation logic
subtasks.description: Create the actual inhalation screening engine.
subtasks.details: Build the inhalation plugin using the selected screening method and keep the equations explicit and versioned. Separate inhalation logic from generic screening routes to avoid semantic leakage.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate the engine against hand calculations and confirm the code path is only selected for inhalation-tagged scenarios.

subtasks.title: Emit inhalation assumptions, limitations, and route-normalized outputs
subtasks.description: Make inhalation outputs transparent and downstream-safe.
subtasks.details: Serialize inhalation screening results into exposureScenario.v1 with route=inhalation, scenarioClass=inhalation-screening, explicit assumptions, limitations, and provenance. Include any specialized notes that prevent downstream misuse as refined inhalation exposure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run contract tests proving inhalation outputs contain all required tags and route-specific fields.

subtasks.title: Benchmark inhalation scenarios against reference cases
subtasks.description: Demonstrate that inhalation screening behaves credibly in known scenarios.
subtasks.details: Create inhalation benchmark fixtures with documented expected outputs and compare plugin results to reference calculations. Record acceptable tolerance bands and known simplifications.
subtasks.testStrategy: Require benchmark pass/fail thresholds in CI and confirm benchmark metadata capture default-source versions.

subtasks.title: Add inhalation failure-mode and edge-case tests
subtasks.description: Harden the plugin against invalid or misleading requests.
subtasks.details: Add tests for missing ventilation terms, impossible aerosol assumptions, unsupported inhalation product categories, and route mismatch. Ensure failures are typed, explicit, and never silently rerouted to generic screening logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the edge-case suite and confirm no invalid inhalation request returns a generic screening result.

tasks.title: Implement PBPK handoff export, scenario comparison, and public MCP tool surface
tasks.description: Expose the public tools and result contracts that let ToxClaw and PBPK MCP consume scenarios without custom glue.
tasks.details: Implement the planned tool catalog: build_screening_exposure_scenario, build_aggregate_exposure_scenario, build_inhalation_screening_scenario, export_pbpk_scenario_input, and compare_exposure_scenarios. PBPK handoff export must translate scenario outputs into the minimal normalized dosing object that PBPK MCP expects, without embedding extra product semantics. Scenario comparison must be a first-class machine-readable feature so ToxClaw can justify refinement from screening to refined assumptions or compare component and aggregate scenarios. Register contract metadata, schema resources, and result examples in the MCP surface.
tasks.testStrategy: Run a live MCP instance and validate tool listings, schema references, PBPK handoff payloads, and comparison outputs. Confirm every tool returns predictable errors, assumptions, and scenario-class tags.

subtasks.title: Implement screening, aggregate, and inhalation public tools
subtasks.description: Expose the core scenario builders as first-class MCP tools.
subtasks.details: Wire the screening, aggregate, and inhalation plugins into dedicated public tools with schema-validated requests and responses. Ensure the tools return explicit assumption/provenance data rather than only dose numbers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Write live-tool tests covering each tool's happy path and key validation failures.

subtasks.title: Implement PBPK handoff export
subtasks.description: Create the normalized external-dose contract consumed downstream by PBPK MCP.
subtasks.details: Build export_pbpk_scenario_input to transform scenario outputs into pbpkScenarioInput.v1 with only the normalized dosing semantics, route, timing pattern, and population context required by PBPK MCP. Do not leak product narratives or scenario-builder internals into the PBPK contract.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate exported objects against the published schema and cross-check field compatibility with PBPK MCP expectations.

subtasks.title: Implement machine-readable scenario comparison
subtasks.description: Make refinement and comparison a real computational output, not just prose.
subtasks.details: Build compare_exposure_scenarios to compute structured deltas between two or more scenarios at the assumption, parameter, and output levels. Emit scenarioComparisonRecord.v1 with both numeric differences and declared methodological differences.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test comparisons for screening vs aggregate, screening vs inhalation, and altered-defaults cases, confirming both numeric and assumption deltas are captured.

subtasks.title: Register schemas, examples, and contract metadata in the MCP surface
subtasks.description: Make the module self-describing for ToxClaw and other clients.
subtasks.details: Add schema resources, example resources, and a machine-readable contract manifest linking each public tool to its response schemas. Ensure all public tools expose response schema references or equivalent metadata.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run resource-discovery tests against a live MCP instance and validate manifest integrity.

subtasks.title: Add future-safe result/status semantics without forcing async now
subtasks.description: Keep the public surface extensible for later probabilistic engines.
subtasks.details: Publish lightweight status or result metadata conventions so later async population engines can reuse the same contract style. Ensure current synchronous tools still return stable metadata and do not require a queue.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the tool responses and confirm the future-safe fields do not burden current synchronous use cases or break simplicity.

tasks.title: Integrate Exposure Scenario MCP with ToxClaw, CompTox MCP, and PBPK MCP
tasks.description: Make the module a first-class line-of-evidence producer and external-dose engine inside the existing ToxMCP stack.
tasks.details: Add suite-level examples and adapters showing how CompTox identity and supporting metadata enter the scenario engine, how scenario outputs map into ToxClaw evidence envelopes and line-of-evidence records, and how PBPK MCP consumes pbpkScenarioInput.v1 without custom transformation code. Publish integration guidance that clearly states the boundary: this module owns external dose construction, PBPK MCP owns internal exposure, and ToxClaw owns WoE, BER, refinement decisions, and final reporting.
tasks.testStrategy: Run cross-suite integration fixtures proving that CompTox-enriched requests build successfully, ToxClaw can ingest outputs as evidence, and PBPK MCP can consume exported scenario objects without bespoke mapping.

subtasks.title: Bind CompTox identity and supporting metadata into scenario requests
subtasks.description: Reuse Tier-0 evidence without duplicating federation logic.
subtasks.details: Add optional adapters or helper flows that allow CompTox chemicalIdentityRecord and supporting physicochemical/use metadata to populate scenario requests or defaults selection. Keep this additive and provenance-rich, not mandatory.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test scenario construction with and without CompTox enrichment and confirm identity references are preserved correctly.

subtasks.title: Map scenario outputs into ToxClaw evidence envelopes and LoE semantics
subtasks.description: Make exposure scenarios consumable by the WoE-centric orchestrator.
subtasks.details: Wrap or annotate exposureScenario, aggregateExposureSummary, and scenarioComparisonRecord outputs with the fields ToxClaw needs for line-of-evidence handling, including source module, context of use, limitations, uncertainty notes, and fit-for-purpose tags.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that ToxClaw can ingest the wrapped outputs without custom transformation logic and preserve provenance links.

subtasks.title: Cross-check pbpkScenarioInput with PBPK MCP contract expectations
subtasks.description: Guarantee a clean downstream dosing handoff.
subtasks.details: Review PBPK MCP's expected scenario semantics and align field names, units, timing semantics, and population context. Add compatibility examples and contract tests that combine an exposure output with PBPK MCP fixtures.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run cross-module tests and confirm that PBPK MCP can validate the exported scenario input directly.

subtasks.title: Add refinement workflow hooks for ToxClaw
subtasks.description: Support the orchestrator without duplicating its decision logic.
subtasks.details: Define how ToxClaw can request scenario comparisons, route-specific recalculation, or aggregate variants while the module remains purely computational. Publish examples of screening-to-refined transitions that preserve all assumption changes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test a ToxClaw-style flow where scenario comparison drives a refinement decision without any scenario math happening inside ToxClaw.

subtasks.title: Publish suite integration guides and boundary docs
subtasks.description: Document how the module fits the wider stack and how not to misuse it.
subtasks.details: Write integration docs showing the CompTox enrichment path, ToxClaw evidence path, PBPK handoff path, and the non-goal that this module does not estimate internal exposure or make risk decisions. Include at least one end-to-end flow diagram.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the guides against the PRD and confirm they never imply ownership of PBPK, WoE, BER, or final recommendation logic.

tasks.title: Execute benchmark validation, documentation hardening, and v0.1.0 release readiness
tasks.description: Turn the implementation into a regulatory-grade first release through benchmark replication, provenance/security review, and complete developer/operator documentation.
tasks.details: Assemble benchmark and edge-case fixtures for deterministic screening, aggregate/co-use, inhalation screening, PBPK handoff export, and scenario comparison. Add schema conformance, benchmark replication, negative-path, and cross-suite integration suites to CI. Complete docs for defaults governance, plugin operations, limitation statements, and provenance policy. Run a security/provenance review, produce a release-readiness report, and only tag v0.1.0 after all acceptance gates pass.
tasks.testStrategy: All benchmark, contract, integration, and regression suites must pass in CI and in a clean local/container environment. The release report must explicitly confirm the v0.1 definition-of-done criteria and known limitations.

subtasks.title: Build benchmark corpus, golden fixtures, and edge-case datasets
subtasks.description: Create the reusable validation corpus for release and regression testing.
subtasks.details: Collect or synthesize deterministic screening cases, aggregate/co-use reference cases, inhalation reference cases, malformed requests, and PBPK handoff examples. Store expected outputs and benchmark notes in versioned fixture directories.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that fixtures cover happy-path, downgraded, and failure-path scenarios and include provenance/source notes.

subtasks.title: Implement benchmark replication and regression suites
subtasks.description: Prove the engine is reproducible and semantically stable.
subtasks.details: Add CI suites that compare live outputs to golden fixtures, validate schemas, enforce route/unit semantics, and verify benchmark deltas remain within approved tolerances. Include negative tests for missing parameters, hidden-default prevention, and impossible scenario combinations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the full regression suite repeatedly and confirm no unexplained drift occurs in numeric results or provenance fields.

subtasks.title: Complete documentation and operator guidance
subtasks.description: Document how to run, interpret, and troubleshoot the module.
subtasks.details: Write API docs, schema catalog, plugin guide, defaults-governance guide, benchmark notes, provenance policy, limitation statements, and troubleshooting docs. Explain scenario-class semantics clearly so downstream users do not overinterpret outputs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the documentation with an engineer unfamiliar with the project and confirm they can run benchmark fixtures and interpret outputs without tribal knowledge.

subtasks.title: Perform security, provenance, and release-readiness reviews
subtasks.description: Audit the module before calling it regulatory-grade or world-class.
subtasks.details: Review defaults integrity, artifact and output hashing, access controls, logging, source citation completeness, and release metadata. Produce a release-readiness checklist confirming every output carries required provenance and every public tool/resource matches the contract manifest.
subtasks.testStrategy: Conduct a formal review and ensure all critical findings are fixed or explicitly documented with rationale before release.

subtasks.title: Tag and publish the v0.1.0 release package
subtasks.description: Ship the first production-grade version of the module.
subtasks.details: Prepare release notes, migration notes, benchmark summary, and conformance report. Tag v0.1.0 only after all release gates pass and publish machine-readable release metadata with schema versions, defaults-registry versions, and known limitations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the tagged release reproduces the same benchmark and contract results in a clean environment before publication.

metadata.description: TaskMaster plan generated from the Exposure Scenario MCP PRD with regulatory-grade scope, subtasks, and dependencies.
